items.headline: Council votes on nine new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: The city is considering a subdivision plan to split a 1-acre vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the Council will decide whether to approve the project.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment project advances at El Camino Real
items.summary: Tree replacement work is underway for the Palo Alto Greens HOA as part of the larger Summerhill redevelopment at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals continued progress on a significant residential/mixed-use project that will reshape the corridor.

items.headline: City Council approves financing for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View is moving forward with a new public safety building, having approved lease revenue bonds to fund the project on April 28. This represents a significant capital investment in city infrastructure that will affect public services and local taxes.

items.headline: Mountain View developing biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan on April 28, which will guide how Mountain View manages trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat. This plan could affect neighborhood character, park development, and environmental policies across the city.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes plus retail on 4.22 acres
items.summary: Sunnyvale Planning Commission reviewed a special development permit for a project combining 55 townhomes, 12 apartment flats, and 18,535 square feet of retail space (including the existing Speedy's Tacos). The project would be subdivided into 17 lots and represents significant residential and commercial infill development in the city.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered an ordinance to revise business license tax rates in the municipal code. This annual update affects local business licensing fees and will take effect in the new fiscal year.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City allows giant promotional signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara approved temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The ordinance permits large-scale promotional displays for a limited time period surrounding the special sporting events.

items.headline: New firefighters contract approved for 2026-2029
items.summary: The City Council authorized a three-year Memorandum of Understanding with the Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171). The contract covers compensation and working conditions for the city's firefighting personnel through 2029.
